steps.title: Set f(x) equal to zero to find x-intercepts
steps.content:
To find where the graph meets the $x$-axis, set $$f(x) = x(x-10)^2(x-11)^3 = 0$$

The polynomial is already in factored form, so we can identify roots directly without additional solving.

steps.title: Apply zero product property and count distinct roots
steps.content:
By the zero product property, when a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero. Each distinct factor gives a root:
  • $x = 0$ (from factor $x$)
  • $x = 10$ (from factor $(x-10)^2$)
  • $x = 11$ (from factor $(x-11)^3$)
We have three distinct solutions. Each corresponds to one $x$-intercept on the graph. That matches choice D — D is correct.
